paper.title: Blue extreme disk-runaway stars with Gaia EDR3

extraction.summary: Paper presents 30 extreme disk-runaway stars; among them 10 are treated as possibly unbound to the Milky Way: S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, B481, HVS 7, LAMOST HVS 1, LAMOST HVS 2, LAMOST HVS 4, J0136+2425, HVS 8. The remaining 20 are either bound or not discussed as unbound and are excluded.

method_chain.summary: Gaia EDR3 proper motions and positions for all 30 program stars.

source_refs.context: With proper motions of significantly improved accuracy and precision from the Gaia early data release 3

method_chain.summary: Spectrophotometric distances derived from previous and new spectrophotometric analyses (see Section 3).

source_refs.context: we used spectrophotometric distance estimates as input for our kinematic investigation

method_chain.summary: Radial velocities measured from model spectra fitting during quantitative spectral analysis.

source_refs.context: radial velocity v_rad were determined by fitting ... model spectra

method_chain.summary: Combine Gaia EDR3 proper motions, spectrophotometric distances, and radial velocities to build 6D phase-space catalogue.

source_refs.context: The spectrophotometric distances and radial velocities were combined with proper motions from Gaia EDR3

method_chain.summary: Adopt solar Galactocentric distance R0=8.3 kpc and solar peculiar motion (U,V,W) from Schönrich et al. (2010); the paper does not give explicit numerical values for U,V,W.

parameters.name: solar_motion_u
parameters.description: Adopted from Schönrich et al. 2010, not given in paper

parameters.name: solar_motion_v
parameters.description: Adopted from Schönrich et al. 2010, not given in paper

parameters.name: solar_motion_w
parameters.description: Adopted from Schönrich et al. 2010, not given in paper

source_refs.context: Uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest (Schönrich et al. 2010) are treated analogously.

method_chain.summary: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013), a three-component axisymmetric potential (bulge, disk, halo).
outputs: Galactic potential and escape velocity field

source_refs.context: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)

method_chain.summary: Compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x,y,z), velocities (v_x,v_y,v_z), and Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf from observables and solar motion.

source_refs.context: Table 3 header includes x,y,z, v_x, v_y, v_z, v_Grf

method_chain.summary: Monte Carlo backward orbit integration in Galactic potential, yielding plane-crossing positions (x_p, y_p, z_p, r_p), velocities (v_xp, v_yp, v_zp, v_Grf,p), and flight time (tau_flight).

source_refs.context: numerically solved ... Monte Carlo simulation ... derived properties at plane intersection

method_chain.summary: Compute local escape velocity v_esc and bound probability P_b using the Galactic potential and the star's current Galactocentric position (from step-07).

source_refs.context: The probability P_b is the fraction of Monte Carlo runs for which the star is bound to the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Subgroup classification of origin (Galactic centre / disk / outer rim) based on galactocentric radius at plane intersection (r_p) from orbit integration.

source_refs.context: Three subgroups may be identified in our sample: stars originating in the GC, bona fide disk runaways, and stars that were presumably ejected from the outer rim of the Galactic disk.

method_chain.summary: Identify gravitationally unbound stars (HVSs/hyper-runaway stars) based on bound probability and origin.

source_refs.context: They include HVS 5 and HVS 6, which are both gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.
HVS 5 is clearly gravitationally unbound ... LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are hyper-runaway stars ... HVS 6 is unbound ... B481 and HVS 7 might be.

method_chain.summary: Determine atmospheric and stellar parameters (Teff, logg, vsini, M, R, logL, age) via model atmosphere fitting and comparison to evolutionary tracks.
outputs: Teff, logg, vsini, mass, radius, logL, age

source_refs.context: Atmospheric parameters ... were determined by fitting ... Stellar masses M and ages tau_evol were inferred

method_chain.summary: Compute ejection velocity v_ej (Galactic rest-frame velocity relative to the rotating Galactic disk) from plane-crossing velocity v_Grf,p and kinematical model.

source_refs.context: the ejection velocity v_ej (defined as the Galactic rest-frame velocity relative to the rotating Galactic disk)

inclusion_assessment.summary: S5-HVS 1 is the most extreme object, gravitationally unbound, consistent with Hills mechanism origin from GC. Paper explicitly states it is unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states it is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.

source_refs.context: it is also the only object that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.
S5-HVS 1 travels at ... and most likely originates in the GC

citation_context_refs.context: S5-HVS 1 travels at 1755±50 km/s and most likely originates in the GC

spectroscopy.description: Spectrograph used for atmospheric parameters. AAOmega with resolving power 1300 and 10000.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 5 is clearly gravitationally unbound, a hyper-runaway star from the disk, not GC.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states it is clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.

source_refs.context: HVS 5 is clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way
initial sample comprised all 40 stars of the MMT-HVS survey... including HVS 5 and HVS 6

citation_context_refs.context: MMT-HVS survey (Brown et al. 2014) including HVS 5

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 6 is gravitationally unbound, originating from the outer rim of the disk.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper states HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way.

source_refs.context: HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way

citation_context_refs.context: MMT-HVS survey (Brown et al. 2014) including HVS 6

inclusion_assessment.summary: B481 might be unbound to the Milky Way, possibly outer rim runaway.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper says B481 might be unbound, not firmly confirmed.

source_refs.context: MMT-HVS survey (Brown et al. 2014) includes B481

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 7 might be unbound, outer rim disk runaway candidate.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper says HVS 7 might be unbound.

source_refs.context: MMT-HVS survey (Brown et al. 2014) includes HVS 7

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 1 is a hyper-runaway star, gravitationally unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states LAMOST HVS 1 is a hyper-runaway star, which are gravitationally unbound.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars.
four candidate HVSs from the LAMOST survey

citation_context_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 from LAMOST survey (Zheng et al. 2014)

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 2 is a possible hyper-runaway star, depending on Galactic mass model.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Unbound status model-dependent, not firmly established.

source_refs.context: Whether LAMOST HVS 2 ... are hyper-runaway stars depends on the choice of the applied Galactic mass model.

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 4 is a hyper-runaway star, gravitationally unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper states LAMOST HVS 4 is a hyper-runaway star, thus unbound.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Previously proposed potential hyper-runaway star; the paper discusses that its status as a hyper-runaway star depends on the adopted Galactic mass model. For model I, the bound probability is 40%, so it is possibly unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly presents J0136+2425 as a potential hyper-runaway star and discusses its possible unbound status.

source_refs.context: the potential hyper-runaway stars SDSS J013655.91+242546.0 (J0136+2425 for short, Tillich et al. 2009)
Whether LAMOST HVS 2, J0136+2425, and HVS 8 are hyper-runaway stars depends on the choice of the applied Galactic mass model.
the potential hyper-runaway stars SDSS J013655.91+242546.0 (J0136+2425 for short, Tillich et al. 2009)

citation_context_refs.context: the potential hyper-runaway stars SDSS J013655.91+242546.0 (J0136+2425 for short, \citeads{2009A&A...507L..37T})

inclusion_assessment.summary: The paper notes that whether HVS 8 is a hyper-runaway star depends on the Galactic mass model. In model I, the bound probability is 55%, making it possibly unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly discusses HVS 8 as a candidate whose hyper-runaway status depends on the model choice, and the bound probability is 55%.

source_refs.context: Whether LAMOST HVS 2, J0136+2425, and HVS 8 are hyper-runaway stars depends on the choice of the applied Galactic mass model.
Our initial sample comprised all 40 stars of the MMT-HVS survey that were revised by Kreuzer et al. 2020...

candidate_groups_considered.description: Program stars from Table 1 that are not claimed to be unbound to the Milky Way: B537, B576, B598, B434, B733, B711, HVS17, B485, HIP114569, PHL346, HIP60350, EC04420-1908, PHL2018, LAMOST HVS3, B143, HD271791, EC19596-5356, BD-2 3766, HIP56322, PG1610+062.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper does not present these objects as possibly unbound; they are either explicitly bound (HD271791, HIP60350) or not discussed as unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Discussion of bound stars: HD271791 and HIP60350 are clearly bound; PG1610+062 is a disk runaway but not claimed unbound.
Table 1 lists all 30 program stars; only a subset are identified as unbound in the text.
